data_flow_trace.original: genesis-tool inserts runtime bytecode + balance at Governance address; constructor NEVER runs; storage = Default::default()
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: deploy_bsc_style() calls db.insert_account_info(target_address, AccountInfo { code: Some(Bytecode::new_raw(runtime_bytecode)), balance, ..AccountInfo::default() }) in a loop over CONTRACTS. 'Governance' is in CONTRACTS at genesis-tool/src/utils.rs:116. No constructor invocation occurs — only runtime bytecode is written, storage left default (all zero).
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Applies to every entry in CONTRACTS array, including Governance at GOVERNANCE_ADDR = 0x...1625F3000.

data_flow_trace.original: Governance.sol constructor calls Ownable(initialOwner) which would SSTORE slot 0, but constructor never runs in BSC-style genesis
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Governance constructor: `constructor(address initialOwner) Ownable(initialOwner) { }`. OZ Ownable (node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/access/Ownable.sol:38-43) requires initialOwner != 0 and calls _transferOwnership(initialOwner) which SSTOREs slot 0 with _owner. Because BSC-style genesis writes only runtime bytecode (no CREATE opcode), _transferOwnership is never executed and slot 0 remains zero.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Constructor execution requires contract creation via CREATE/CREATE2; deploying raw runtime bytecode bypasses it entirely.

data_flow_trace.original: Genesis.sol imports GovernanceConfig but never calls anything on SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE itself
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Genesis.initialize() executes 7 phases: configs (GovernanceConfig.initialize only — NOT Governance), oracles, pools, validator management, performance tracker, reconfiguration, blocker. There is NO transferOwnership / acceptOwnership / addExecutor / initialize call targeting SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE (0x...1625F3000). Grep of src/ confirms Governance is only used as a PRIVILEGED CALLER (requireAllowed(SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE)) in other contracts, never as a callee during Genesis.

data_flow_trace.original: genesis-tool/src/genesis.rs has no code path that seeds slot 0 of GOVERNANCE_ADDR with an owner address
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: GenesisConfig has NO field for governance owner. SolGenesisInitParams (genesis.rs:328-341) carries validator configs, oracle configs, JWK configs, validators, etc. — no governanceOwner/governanceAdmin parameter. The only storage-seeding mechanism (verify.rs:141-147 db.insert_account_storage) is used in Verify mode, not Generate. In Generate, after execute_revm_sequential (execute.rs:162), bundle_state contains only SSTOREs performed by Genesis.initialize — which makes no call to GOVERNANCE_ADDR.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: No config field exists to pass a governance owner; no code path sets slot 0 of GOVERNANCE_ADDR.

data_flow_trace.original: Any post-genesis caller invoking addExecutor(executor) hits onlyOwner which reads slot 0 == 0 and reverts OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(msg.sender)
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: addExecutor is marked `external onlyOwner`. onlyOwner -> _checkOwner() -> `if (owner() != _msgSender()) revert OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(_msgSender())`. With _owner==0 and msg.sender being any real EOA/contract (never 0 in a normal tx), this ALWAYS reverts. Same for removeExecutor.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: No bypass modifier, no system-caller shortcut. Note: SYSTEM_CALLER = 0x...1625F0000, not address(0), so even system-triggered calls fail.

data_flow_trace.original: No executor can ever be added → execute() reverts for every caller → all SUCCEEDED proposals are un-executable
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: execute(uint64,address[],bytes[]) has `onlyExecutor` modifier: `if (!_executors.contains(msg.sender)) revert Errors.NotExecutor(msg.sender)`. Since _executors (EnumerableSet) is never populated (addExecutor unreachable), the set is empty => every execute() call reverts. No alternative execution path exists in the contract.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Only owner can grow _executors via addExecutor; owner is unreachable.

data_flow_trace.original: Recovery impossible: transferOwnership / acceptOwnership from Ownable2Step also require onlyOwner
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: transferOwnership is `onlyOwner` — blocked by same _owner==0. acceptOwnership requires msg.sender == _pendingOwner; _pendingOwner (slot 1, offset 0) is also zero (never set), so msg.sender != 0 fails there too. renounceOwnership is overridden in Governance.sol:85-87 to revert OperationNotSupported() regardless.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: No backdoor; every ownership-mutation function requires a privileged principal that does not exist.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: author is slightly imprecise (acceptOwnership checks pendingOwner, not onlyOwner) but the end result — no recovery possible — holds because _pendingOwner is also zero.

reasoning_chain: Every step of the data flow is independently verified against current code. Step 1: genesis-tool's deploy_bsc_style (execute.rs:47-54) writes only runtime bytecode + balance to Governance's canonical address (GOVERNANCE_ADDR in utils.rs:116). Step 2: Governance.sol:63-65 has an empty constructor body that only runs Ownable's initialization via inheritance; since the constructor opcode is never executed in BSC-style genesis, the SSTORE of slot 0 (_owner) never happens. Step 3: Genesis.sol:180-218 initializes GovernanceConfig but makes zero calls to SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE. Step 4: GenesisConfig (genesis.rs:30-75) contains NO field for governance owner, and no code path in genesis-tool writes to slot 0 of GOVERNANCE_ADDR. Steps 5/6: the onlyOwner guard at Governance.sol:555-557 (addExecutor) combined with OZ Ownable's _checkOwner (Ownable.sol:63-67) makes addExecutor permanently unreachable, leaving _executors empty, which makes every execute() call fail onlyExecutor (Governance.sol:72-77, 482-486). Step 7: Ownable2Step acceptOwnership requires pendingOwner==msg.sender (Ownable2Step.sol:60-66) but pendingOwner is also zero, and renounceOwnership is overridden to revert (Governance.sol:85-87). The weakest link does not exist; there is no guard that breaks the attack. The test harness (test/unit/governance/Governance.t.sol:110-121) explicitly uses vm.load + vm.store to copy slot 0 and slot 1 from a temporary `new Governance(owner)` deployment — definitive evidence that developers recognize the constructor is required and that the production path omits this seeding.

key_evidence.why_matters: Owner initialization lives exclusively in the constructor, which does not run when runtime bytecode is written directly to an address.
Proves genesis-tool writes runtime bytecode + balance only; no constructor, no storage seeding, applies to Governance via CONTRACTS array.
Governance IS in the BSC-style deploy set, confirming it receives runtime-only deployment.
GovernanceConfig is initialized, but Governance itself is NEVER touched during Genesis.initialize. Confirms gap.
Only gate to populate _executors set; blocked by onlyOwner with _owner=0.
execute() gated by onlyExecutor; empty _executors set => all executes revert.

The unit test itself acknowledges that when Governance is placed at SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE via vm.etch (no constructor), slots 0/1 must be manually copied. The production genesis-tool has no equivalent copy, so on-chain _owner stays zero.

counterargument: The strongest argument against EXISTS would be: perhaps the gravity-reth node (outside this repo) applies a hardcoded state override at chain start that seeds slot 0 of 0x...1625F3000 with a governance owner, bypassing Genesis.initialize entirely — similar to how BSC seeds system-contract state. This repo only contains the Solidity + Rust genesis-tool, not the Reth node itself, so we cannot prove the node doesn't do this. However, (a) the genesis-tool's stated output (genesis_accounts.json + genesis_contracts.json) is what the node consumes; if the node does NOT consume those outputs verbatim, the genesis-tool's storage-seeding logic is dead code — which contradicts the visible bundle_state serialization and verify.rs pattern. (b) There is no hook, config, or marker in the Solidity/Rust sources that tells the node which address should own Governance, making an ad-hoc node-side seeding unlikely. (c) The test harness's explicit vm.store workaround is the decisive tell: if the node seeded slot 0, the test could have followed the same pattern (e.g., read from a config). The counterargument therefore relies on unwitnessed out-of-repo code and is weaker than the direct evidence.
related_existing_tests: test/unit/governance/Governance.t.sol::GovernanceTest::setUp (manual vm.store slot0/slot1 seeding, no production-path equivalent)
